title: The Highland Welcome.
content:
When Death's dark stream I ferry o'er,
            A time that surely shall come;
        In Heaven itself I'll ask no more
            Than just a Highland welcome.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Burns, titled "The Highland Welcome.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
